mcqs.question: In a CUDA block with 256 threads performing tree-based reduction, at which stride value do the remaining active threads first fit entirely within a single warp?
mcqs.explanation: When the stride is 32, only threads 0-31 are active. Since a warp consists of 32 consecutive threads, threads 0-31 all belong to warp 0, and lockstep execution means __syncthreads() is no longer needed.

mcqs.question: What does the warp shuffle instruction __shfl_down_sync(0xFFFFFFFF, val, 8) do for thread 3?

options.text: Thread 3 sends its value to thread 11
Thread 3 receives the value of val from thread 11
Thread 3 receives the value of val from thread 0
Thread 3 sends its value to shared memory index 8

mcqs.explanation: __shfl_down_sync with delta 8 causes each thread i to receive the register value from thread i + delta. So thread 3 reads the val register from thread 3 + 8 = thread 11. No shared memory is involved.

mcqs.question: Why is the volatile keyword needed for shared memory pointers when __syncthreads() is removed in the warp-level reduction (pre-Volta)?

options.text: To allocate shared memory dynamically at runtime
To prevent the compiler from caching shared memory values in registers, ensuring threads see each other's writes
To make shared memory accessible from the host CPU
To enable atomic operations on shared memory locations

mcqs.explanation: Without __syncthreads(), the compiler may optimize shared memory reads by keeping values in registers. The volatile qualifier forces every read and write to go directly to shared memory, ensuring visibility of updates between threads in the same warp.

mcqs.question: In the two-phase warp-shuffle reduction for a 256-thread block, how many calls to __syncthreads() are needed?
mcqs.explanation: The two-phase approach needs only 1 __syncthreads() -- after all warps write their partial sums to shared memory (end of Phase 1) and before warp 0 reads those values for the final reduction (start of Phase 2). Intra-warp shuffles need no synchronization.

mcqs.question: What is the primary advantage of warp shuffle instructions over shared memory for intra-warp reduction?

options.text: Warp shuffles can operate across different thread blocks
Warp shuffles transfer values directly between registers without shared memory, avoiding bank conflicts and reducing latency
Warp shuffles support double-precision operations while shared memory does not
Warp shuffles automatically handle blocks with non-power-of-2 sizes

mcqs.explanation: Warp shuffle instructions allow threads within a warp to directly exchange register values without going through shared memory. This eliminates shared memory bank conflicts and reduces latency from ~5 cycles (shared memory) to ~1 cycle (shuffle).
